failed_assumptions: RAVEL probes on target model can be trained with available models
GPT-2 Medium can substitute for Gemma 2B for geographic probe training
candidates.notes: Probe quality gate FAILS: best probe (city-continent, GPT-2 Medium layer 20) achieves 66.2% (7 classes) vs gate of 85%. Root cause: Gemma 2B and Llama-3.1-8B are both HF-gated; no available bridge model encodes geographic knowledge at sufficient quality. Shuffled control = real absorption rate (gap=0.0 for all configs): random projection from d=1024 to d=2304 destroys directional signal. Paper framing: R4-B should be reported as 'proper probes require target model access; RAVEL cross-domain evidence remains conditional pending Gemma 2B authentication.'

blocking_issues: Gemma 2B HF-gated: cannot train probes on target model
Llama-3.1-8B HF-gated: cannot use as fallback for probe training
Best available bridge (GPT-2 Medium layer 20): 66.2% city-continent (gate: 85%)
Random projection d=1024->d=2304 destroys directional signal (shuffled=real)

paper_implications.H3_status: UNRESOLVED - proper probes require HF-gated model access
paper_implications.ravel_cross_domain_framing: RAVEL cross-domain absorption rates from R3 are unreliable (wrong probe model). R4-B confirms: probe quality gate cannot be met without target model. Paper should report R3 RAVEL results with prominent caveat and position as 'preliminary cross-domain evidence pending proper probe training.'
paper_implications.intra_ravel_coherence: R3 rho=0.924 is also suspect (Qwen probe projection artifact). R4-B cannot compute meaningful intra-RAVEL rho without proper probes.
